2603.30002.title: Tracking Equivalent Mechanistic Interpretations Across Neural Networks
2603.30002.paper_summary: Mechanistic interpretability (MI) is an emerging framework for interpreting neural networks. Given a task and model, MI aims to discover a succinct algorithmic process, an interpretation, that explains the model's decision process on that task. However, MI is difficult to scale and generalize. This stems in part from two key challenges: there is no precise notion of a valid interpretation; and, generating interpretations is often an ad hoc process. In this paper, we address these challenges by defining and studying the problem of interpretive equivalence: determining whether two different models share a common interpretation, without requiring an explicit description of what that interpretation is. At the core of our approach, we propose and formalize the principle that two interpretations of a model are equivalent if all of their possible implementations are also equivalent. We develop an algorithm to estimate interpretive equivalence and case study its use on Transformer-based models. To analyze our algorithm, we introduce necessary and sufficient conditions for interpretive equivalence based on models' representation similarity. We provide guarantees that simultaneously relate a model's algorithmic interpretations, circuits, and representations. Our framework lays a foundation for the development of more rigorous evaluation methods of MI and automated, generalizable interpretation discovery methods.
2603.30002.paper_summary_zh: 機械解釋性（MI）是一個新興的框架，用於解釋神經網絡。<lb/>給定一個任務和模型，MI的目標是發現一個簡潔的算法過程，即解釋，來說明模型在該任務上的決策過程。<lb/>然而，MI難以擴展和泛化。<lb/>這部分源於兩個主要挑戰：沒有對有效解釋的精確概念；而且，生成解釋通常是一個臨時的過程。<lb/>在本文中，我們通過定義和研究解釋等價性問題來解決這些挑戰：確定兩個不同模型是否共享一個共同的解釋，而不需要明確描述該解釋是什麼。<lb/>我們的方法核心在於，我們提出並形式化了這樣一個原則：如果模型的所有可能實現都是等價的，則該模型的兩個解釋是等價的。<lb/>我們開發了一種算法來估計解釋等價性，並對其在基於Transformer的模型上的使用進行案例研究。<lb/>為了分析我們的算法，我們基於模型的表示相似性引入了解釋等價性的必要和充分條件。<lb/>我們提供了保證，這些保證同時涉及模型的算法解釋、電路和表示。<lb/>我們的框架為開發更嚴謹的MI評估方法和自動化、可泛化的解釋發現方法奠定了基礎。
2603.30002.author: Alan Sun et.al.
